title: IS428 Visual Analytics & Business Intelligence Project
text: Mini-Challenge 2 presents a visual analytics problem centered on bias, incomplete evidence, and conflicting narratives. Two interest groups, FILAH and TROUT, accuse the COOTEFOO committee of opposing forms of economic bias, yet both rely on partial datasets. This creates an ideal setting for visual analytics because the challenge is not just to inspect data, but to understand how different data samples can produce different stories. Our project helps a non-technical journalist explore these competing claims transparently, identify whether bias exists, and distinguish genuine behavioral patterns from distortions caused by missing or selective evidence.

title: IS428 Visual Analytics & Business Intelligence Project
IS428 Visual Analytics & Business Intelligence Project
text:
Data
The project uses the MC2 knowledge graph datasets: FILAH.json, TROUT.json, and journalist.json. These are built from two main sources: meeting notes and travel records. The graph contains entities such as people, organisations, places, meetings, discussions, plans, topics, and trips, with attributes including date, time, role, sentiment, industry, latitude, and longitude. These datasets are linked through shared person and place records, allowing comparison between partial and complete views of the same committee activities.

title: IS428 Visual Analytics & Business Intelligence Project
text:
Scope of Work
Our scope covers four main components.

1. Data Preprocessing and Preparation
The JSON knowledge graph datasets (FILAH, TROUT, and journalist datasets) will be preprocessed and transformed into relational tables suitable for analysis in Tableau. This includes extracting nodes and edges, cleaning attributes such as dates, locations, and sentiment values, and structuring the data to enable efficient querying and visualization.


2. Dashboard Design and Development
Interactive Tableau dashboards will be designed to support multiple analytical perspectives. These include dashboards for committee overview, member-level behavior, travel and location patterns, topic and sentiment analysis, and comparisons between the partial datasets (FILAH and TROUT) and the full journalist dataset.


3. Visual Analytical Workflow Development
We will develop visual analytic workflows that allow users to interactively explore the data and trace accusations back to the supporting or missing evidence. These workflows will enable users to examine differences between datasets, investigate individual committee members, and identify patterns that may indicate bias or incomplete information.


4. Analysis and Interpretation of Findings
The final stage will synthesize analytical findings into a clear narrative explaining whether observed bias in COOTEFOO member behavior is genuine, exaggerated, or influenced by sampling limitations. The project will focus on explanation, comparison, and interpretive visual analysis rather than predictive modeling.
